proposed_benchmarks.name: KinDER
proposed_benchmarks.paper_title: KinDER: A Physical Reasoning Benchmark for Robot Learning and Planning
proposed_benchmarks.what: 25 procedurally generated environments + Gymnasium-compatible Python library + 13 baselines spanning task and motion planning, imitation learning. Targets reasoning over embodiment / environment / task constraints.
proposed_benchmarks.risk_note: 13 baseline coverage of paradigms (TAMP / IL / LLM-based) needs balance check; community standard adoption uncertain.

proposed_benchmarks.name: XTC-Bench
proposed_benchmarks.paper_title: Beyond Accuracy: Benchmarking Cross-Task Consistency in Unified Multimodal Models
proposed_benchmarks.domain: Unified Multimodal Models cross-task semantic consistency
proposed_benchmarks.what: Scene-graph-grounded evaluation framework that derives both generation prompts and understanding queries from the same scene graph, measuring whether unified MM stays semantically consistent across understanding/generation tasks for the same visual concept.
proposed_benchmarks.risk_note: Scene-graph derivation pipeline biases evaluation toward graph-amenable concepts; coverage of free-form generation unclear.

proposed_benchmarks.name: M3-VQA
proposed_benchmarks.paper_title: M$^3$-VQA: A Benchmark for Multimodal, Multi-Entity, Multi-Hop Visual Question Answering
proposed_benchmarks.what: VQA bench requiring reasoning across multiple entities and multimodal evidence chains, beyond single-image/single-entity QA.
proposed_benchmarks.risk_note: Multi-hop chain construction quality and shortcut prevention need scrutiny.

proposed_benchmarks.name: FCMBench-Video
proposed_benchmarks.what: Targets video documents (slides, lectures, screencasts) where temporal + structural document layout matters jointly.
proposed_benchmarks.risk_note: Niche but well-defined domain; representativeness across document types needs check.

proposed_benchmarks.name: DRAGON
proposed_benchmarks.paper_title: DRAGON: A Benchmark for Evidence-Grounded Visual Reasoning over Diagrams
proposed_benchmarks.what: Forces models to ground reasoning steps in specific diagram regions/elements rather than free-form text reasoning.
proposed_benchmarks.risk_note: Evidence-grounding annotation cost limits scale; may favor models with explicit attention/grounding heads.

proposed_benchmarks.name: OmniVTG
proposed_benchmarks.paper_title: OmniVTG: A Large-Scale Dataset and Training Paradigm for Open-World Video Temporal Grounding
proposed_benchmarks.what: Large-scale dataset + training paradigm for grounding text queries to video temporal segments in open-world scenarios.
proposed_benchmarks.risk_note: Open-world claim requires demonstration of generalization beyond training distribution.

proposed_benchmarks.name: QCalEval
proposed_benchmarks.paper_title: QCalEval: Benchmarking Vision-Language Models for Quantum Calibration Plot Understanding
proposed_benchmarks.what: Targets a niche scientific evaluation domain — VLM ability to read and reason about quantum calibration plots.
proposed_benchmarks.risk_note: Very narrow domain; valuable for scientific VLM but limited generalization signal.

notable_datasets.name: OmniVTG
notable_datasets.what: Large-scale open-world video temporal grounding dataset paired with new training paradigm.

note: 이번주 추적 벤치(ScanNet++·LIBERO·nuScenes·MMMU·VBench 등)에서 새 SOTA 수치 보고는 잡힌 게 없음. 대신 '새 평가 framework·새 카테고리 정의' 흐름이 강함 — KinDER(physical reasoning), XTC-Bench(unified MM cross-task), M3-VQA(multi-hop multimodal), DRAGON(diagram evidence-grounding) 등이 한 날 나란히 등장. 평가 인프라가 새 카테고리로 분기하는 단계.
